scope: Capability-facing comparison between canonical API1 tid=431 and the currently confirmed noncanonical shell families 453/476/483/506/537 using the repository demos plus focused raw follow-up evidence
verdict: Current evidence no longer supports reading API1 as only one canonical positive branch plus one probe shell. The current anonymous map now contains at least five thinner noncanonical success-shell families alongside canonical tid=431.
Canonical tid=431 remains the only caller-default branch that stably exposes a non-empty cover shell together with non-empty video_ids across the current 3-CID public matrix.
tid=453 is the richest noncanonical API1 shell currently confirmed: it keeps a non-empty cover title and type while aggregated_video_ids_count stays 0 across the current 3-CID matrix.

tid=483 is thinner than 431 and 453 but thicker than the empty-valued shell families: on the current 3-CID matrix it keeps top-level success and non-empty video_ids materialization while cover title/type/pay_status stay empty.

tid=476, tid=506, and tid=537 all remain in the success-shell-without-sample family on the current 3-CID demo matrix, but the dedicated 2026-06-23 live validation now splits tid=476 from tid=506 at the raw-field-family layer: tid=476 is an imgtag-heavy ultra-thin shell with qbox_imgtag={}, while tid=506 is a 10-field empty-valued field shell with a smaller 9-key extra-field family and no non-empty extras.

431.current_shape: non-empty cover shell plus non-empty video_ids family

453.current_shape: non-empty cover title/type shell with aggregated_video_ids_count=0

483.current_shape: video_ids materialize while cover title/type/pay_status stay empty

476.current_shape: top-level success with no video_ids and no stable caller-facing cover fields in the demo matrix; dedicated demo/raw validation now exposes a 25-key imgtag family led by qbox_imgtag={}

506.current_shape: top-level success with an empty-valued 10-field shell, a 9-key extra-field family, and no stable caller-facing cover fields in the demo matrix

537.current_shape: top-level success without sample-bearing cover materialization

documentation_rule: Keep tid=431 as the stable caller-default recipe when the caller expects a cover shell plus video_ids.
Expose tid=453 as the strongest currently confirmed noncanonical cover-only shell.
Describe tid=483 as a video_ids-led thin success shell rather than a canonical positive branch.
Describe tid=476 as an imgtag-family ultra-thin success shell, distinct from the current tid=506 empty-valued field shell.
Describe tid=506 and tid=537 as thinner success-shell families for branch mapping, not as direct caller-default replacements.
remaining_open_questions: Whether tid=483 ever materializes non-empty cover title/type on broader samples or under different environments remains open.
Whether any of these noncanonical API1 shell families change thickness under aged-cookie or login-state replay remains open.
